note: Frozen malformed/hostile retrieval fixtures for verify:p8. Committed files are digest-pinned bytes; generatedPayloads freeze exact generator parameters and payload digests so the implementation lane cannot author the attacks it must survive. Compressed encodings of generated payloads may vary by encoder, but decompressed payload digests and size thresholds are exact. adversarialCaseId binds each fixture to its expected outcome in ../adversarial/manifest.json; size limits reference ../thresholds.json retrievalLimits.

files.attack: truncated JSON with embedded control bytes
declared text/html; actual bytes are a PDF document
invalid xref offsets, absurd stream length, truncated trailer

generatedPayloads.generator: any RFC 1952 gzip encoding of repeat byte 0x00
generatedPayloads.note: decompressed payload is exactly one byte over retrievalLimits.maxDecompressedBytes; the limit must trip before full decompression

generatedPayloads.generator:
repeat '
' 200000 times, append 'parser-resource-exhaustion', then repeat '
' 200000 times
generatedPayloads.note: deeply nested HTML payload used to exercise parser CPU/memory/time/output fences without committing a multi-megabyte fixture file
